equations.name: Ohm's Law
equations.cross_domain: Isomorphic to F=ma (2n pure multiplication family). Also: Fourier's heat law Q=kA*dT/dx, Fick's diffusion J=D*dC/dx.

equations.name: Newton's Second Law
equations.cross_domain: Same structure as V=IR, p=mv, W=Pt. Universal 2n multiplication pair.

equations.name: Kinetic Energy
equations.cost_breakdown: pow(v, 2) = 3n; mul(m, pow(v,2)) = 2n; mul(0.5, product) = 2n. Total = 7n. (0.5 treated as precomputed constant literal.)
equations.cross_domain: Rotational KE = 0.5*I*omega^2 is structurally identical (7n). Spring potential PE = 0.5*k*x^2 is also identical (7n).

equations.name: Newton's Law of Gravitation
equations.cost_breakdown: pow(r, 2) = 3n; div(m, pow(r,2)) = 2n; mul(M, div(...)) = 2n; mul(G, product) = 2n. Total = 9n.
equations.cross_domain: Isomorphic to Coulomb's law F=kq1q2/r^2 (same 9n structure). Both are inverse-square laws sharing identical circuit graph.

equations.name: Wave Speed
equations.cross_domain: 2n multiplication pair family: identical structure to V=IR, F=ma, p=mv.

equations.name: Hooke's Law
equations.cross_domain: The neg wrapper adds 2n to any 2n product. Pattern: neg(mul(a,b)) = 4n. Common in restoring-force contexts.

equations.name: Electric Power (simple form)
equations.cost_breakdown: mul(V, I) = 2n. Note: P=I^2*R costs 5n (pow=3n + mul=2n); P=V^2/R costs 5n (pow=3n + div=2n). Simplest form P=VI reported.
equations.cross_domain: P=VI is the 2n canonical. The 5n forms P=I^2R and P=V^2/R are in the pow-then-scale family.

equations.name: Snell's Law (ratio form)
equations.cost_breakdown: sin() is outside the real EML primitive set. Via complex EML approximation using two sigmoid-based sin approximations (each ~4n via Taylor or CORDIC analog), plus div = 2n: total approximately 9n. In practice, if angles are precomputed: div(sin_theta2, sin_theta1) = 2n straightforward.
equations.cross_domain: If both angles precomputed to real values: reduces to 2n division. The sin-domain penalty is architectural, not inherent to the ratio.

equations.name: Pressure
equations.cross_domain: 1n family member when denominator is variable only: recip(A)*F. As div: 2n. Pattern shared with current density J=I/A, stress sigma=F/A.

equations.name: Work (theta=0 or known angle)
equations.cost_breakdown: If theta is a known constant: mul(F, d) = 2n, then mul(cos_theta_literal, Fd) = 2n. Total = 4n. If theta is variable: cos approximation adds 2n more via sigmoid identity cos(x) = 1-2*sin^2(x/2), total ~6n approximate.
equations.cross_domain: At theta=0: degenerates to 2n pure multiplication. General form is the cos-penalty pattern.

equations.name: Momentum
equations.cross_domain: Exact isomorph of F=ma, V=IR, p=mv, W=Pt. All 2n. The simplest non-trivial computation class.

equations.name: Centripetal Acceleration
equations.cross_domain: 5n pow-then-div family. Related to kinetic energy per unit mass (v^2 factor). Also: angular velocity omega=v/r costs 2n; centripetal force F=mv^2/r costs 7n.

equations.name: Period of Simple Pendulum (small angle)
equations.cost_breakdown: div(L, g) = 2n; sqrt(div(L,g)) = 2n; mul(pi, sqrt) = 2n; mul(2, product) = 2n. Total = 8n. (Treating 2*pi as merged constant: 6n.)
equations.cross_domain: 8n sqrt-family. Same cost as orbital period T=2*pi*sqrt(a^3/GM) modulo one extra mul. The sqrt(ratio) pattern appears in resonant systems universally.

equations.name: Coulomb's Law
equations.cost_breakdown: pow(r, 2) = 3n; div(q2, pow(r,2)) = 2n; mul(q1, div(...)) = 2n; mul(k, product) = 2n. Total = 9n.
equations.cross_domain: Exact isomorph of Newton's gravitational law (9n). Both are three-variable inverse-square laws. The circuit graph is identical — only the physical constants differ.

equations.name: Wien's Displacement Law
equations.cost_breakdown: div(b, T) = 2n. (b = 2.898e-3 m·K is a constant; if treated as ELSb(ln(b), ln(T)): still 2n for the ln-domain division.)

verified_at.note: Solar surface temperature gives peak emission in visible green

equations.cross_domain: 2n division family: Curie law chi=C/T (same structure), Bragg's law d=lambda/(2*sin_theta) (sin penalty), harmonic period f=1/T (1n recip).
